evals.prompt: Catch me up on this week's meetings
evals.expected_output: Fetches all Fireflies transcripts from the past 7 days, runs the master classifier across sub-systems (PD, Ops, Quality, Regulatory, Margin, Intel, Founder), auto-flags urgent items first per Mode 2, then presents the full Action Item Report grouped by primary label with cross-references for multi-label items, ending in a 'which should I push?' confirmation gate.
expectations: Multiple meetings/transcripts from the past week are covered, not just one
Urgent items are surfaced in their own section before the general action item list, per Mode 2
The Action Item Report uses the skill's section structure (e.g. URGENT, QUALITY, REGULATORY, OPS/PD, DECISIONS) rather than a flat meeting-by-meeting recap
Items with more than one sub-system label are split and cross-referenced
No Asana write happens before the 'which should I push?' confirmation

evals.prompt: The formula for the Sorrel lip treatment was approved on our call — log it
evals.expected_output: Finds the relevant meeting/transcript, classifies the approval as carrying pd + plm labels, proposes moving the Sorrel Formula Tracker task to Signed Approvals (which per asana-pd-manager also triggers the IL Review Gate side-effect), and flags the approval for outlook-plm-bridge / asana-plm-bridge cross-flag — presented as a confirmation preview, not an executed change.
expectations: The action item is classified with both a pd label and a plm cross-flag, not just logged as a generic task
The proposed action is specifically a Formula Tracker stage move to Signed Approvals on the Sorrel task
A PLM cross-flag is explicitly mentioned (for the formula approval record)
The output is a confirmation preview awaiting approval, not a claim that the move already happened
If no real matching transcript is found, the output says so plainly rather than fabricating one
